observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned official rectify this accounting practice in order to present fairly the financial statements. Also, correcting entries should be drawn in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the accounts affected. Strict compliance to the provision of the IPSAS is also advised.
observations.managementAction: Conducted Physical Count by Office-In-Charge; other concerned official to rectify the accounting practice as part of consolidation process.
observations.reason: Inventories were not recorded.

observations.recommendation: Free the Acting Accountant from other functions and focus to accounting functions in order to submit the financial reports in accordance with the aforementioned regulations and strictly comply with the prescribed period of report submission so that timely audit of account could be made and avoid sanctions such as withholding of salaries.
observations.managementAction: Free the Acting Accountant from other functions and focus to accounting functions in order to submit the financial reports.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended
that management create an Inventory Committee to come up with a Physical
Inventory Plan for a complete and thorough physical inventory of the agency’s
assets. After which, require the immediate reconciliation with the accounting
records to facilitate review of noted variances, effect necessary adjustments,
provision of necessary disclosure in the notes to financial statements, and the
necessary disposition for those non-existing/missing and disposal of
unserviceable items.

We also recommended that the following procedures be strictly observed:

a.) The Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer to:
• conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records,
• maintain subsidiary records such as property cards and PPE ledger
cards,
b.) Insure all insurable properties to indemnify or compensate for any
damage to, or loss of all its properties in case of destruction; and
c.) Allocate in the annual budget of the Municipality the estimated cost for
titling of land to secure their ownership.

In view of the aforementioned observations, we recommended the following:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare the monthly and detailed Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs)
to reconcile the Cash in Bank records for all of its bank accounts (whether
local or foreign) with the depository bank balances within ten (10) days
from receipt of the bank statements. Regularly and timely submit the BRSs
with complete supporting documents to the concerned COA auditor for
verification;
b. To be updated of any reconciling item and to specify the details of the
unidentified book reconciling items, conduct thorough analysis and review
of cash accounts through the Bank Statements, BRSs and its other
supporting documents as to ascertain the accuracy of the reports. The
book reconciling items noted in the BRSs that are validated by supporting
documents be immediately adjusted to correct the balances of the Cash in
Bank accounts;
c. Make the necessary adjusting entry for any stale-dated check to exclude it
from the list of outstanding checks, as follows:
Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account ₱ XXXXX
Accounts Payable ₱ XXXXX
To the Municipal Treasurer and/or Disbursing Officer:
d. Observe judicious issuance of checks to avoid insufficiency of funds and
submit and furnish to the Municipal Accountant all paid DVs summarized in
the Report of Checks Issued on a daily basis and copies of validated
Deposit Slips summarized in Report on Collections and Deposits to ensure
timely recording of disbursements and deposits in the books of both
offices;
To the Treasurer’s Office:
e. Conduct at least quarterly reconciliation with the Accounting Office on the
Cash in Bank account balances (as well as on the Cash in Local Treasury
account) between the Cashbooks and SLs; and
f. Send written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes
stale, in accordance with the pertinent provisions of MNGAs for LGUs and
Updated LTOM, and notify the payees with stale checks in their possession
for cancellation and/or replacement and ensure that such cancellation and/or
replacement will be reported in the Accounting Office for timely and
appropriate adjustment/s in the books of accounts. The cancelled stale
checks be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of
cancellation.

We recommended that the Management institute measures to improve the
internal control on cash management, recording and monitoring by requiring
the Treasurer’s Office to:
a.) Assign/designate personnel in recording the daily transactions in the
Cashbooks for Cash in Bank, ruling and footing the cashbooks at the end
of each month, and conducting at least monthly reconciliation between the
Cash in Bank balances per cashbook and bank statement, and capacitate
the personnel with the pertinent guidelines on the proper handling, custody
and judicious preparation of the said cashbooks; and
b.) Ensure that the cashbooks are updated daily and conduct regular
reconciliation between cashbook balances and bank balances for all of the
accounts (even in instances that the unadjusted balances are the same)
and monitor and record/adjust any valid cashbook reconciling item in the
Cashbooks for Cash in Bank to reflect the correct cashbook balances of
the respective Cash in Bank accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the Municipal
Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS by adequately
providing a concise narrative on significant changes and breakdown of material
balances to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LDRRMC, through its LDRRM Officer, prepare a
complete and comprehensive annual LDRRMF Investment Plan which shall
include the PPAs of current year appropriations and shall incorporate under a
separate caption the list of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances
of previous years’ LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management:
a.) Strategically plan and implement its developmental projects within the
target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is
optimally utilized;
b.) Specify the implementation target period of the projects in the Annual
Investment Program to guide the Municipality in scheduling the
procurement activities and evaluation of actual performance against
standards on a periodic basis; and
c.) Require the MPDC to conduct periodic monitoring and evaluation of the
municipality's PPA implementation with appropriate reports submitted to
the Mayor for perusal and appropriate action and furnish the audit team of
the required reports for timely review and validation of accomplishments.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point
System-Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) to:
a.) establish clear baselines and realistic estimates in budget preparation
for PPAs translated from the demands/commitments identified in the
GAD agenda;
b.) diligently prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report within the time
frame as required under the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2016-01; and
c.) update and institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and
systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and
policy formulation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer maintain and update subsidiary ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each group/class of PPEs.
observations.managementAction: Opened the position of Inventory GSO.
observations.reason: Election Ban

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the management prepare/issue a memorandum requiring the responsible personnel to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and monitor the compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: MEO provided the cost for each Local Road Assets.
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of adhering to the pertinent provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disbursing Officer regularly rule and foot the cashbooks at the end of each month; coordinate with the Accounting Office and keep copies of the DVs to be maintained and chronologically filed in the MTO in order to facilitate timely and accurate recording of all disbursements in the cashbooks; and emphasize the strict use of pre-numbered PCVs in the grant and liquidation of PCF.
observations.managementAction: Adoption of new system for preparation of PCVs. Copies of DVs are not chronologically filed at the MTO. PCVs were already used however they are not pre-numbered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Budget Officer (i) comply with the provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and (ii) reconcile their records on allotments and obligations monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be immediately verified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the amount reflected in the books of accounting to the budget's SCBAA.
observations.reason: The pertinent disclosures vis-à-vis SCBAA were not provided in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures and to strictly adhere to the disclosure requirements of IPSAS in the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure that the financial reports are effective.
observations.managementAction: Opened the position of GSO and the Acting Municipal Accountant to provide the lacking disclosures when information becomes available.
observations.reason: The management still doesn't have an inventory team to conduct the physical count of the PPE and assess its current fair market value. No trainings were provided to officers on preparation of SCBAA due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management coordinate with the DSWD to improve implementation through timely distribution and increase of manpower; and direct concerned offices to timely and accurately prepare the disbursement voucher and liquidation reports, return the unexpended balance to the concerned source agency and record the return in the books.
observations.managementAction: That the management coordinate with the DSWD to improve the implementation of succeeding programs through timely distribution and increase of manpower to facilitate the process flow and activities involved in the programs; and direct the concerned offices to timely and accurately prepare the disbursement voucher and liquidation reports, return the unexpended balance to the concerned source agency and record the return in the books, as required under the aforementioned rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following: require the Local Finance Committee to timely prepare the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format; require the LFC to accomplish necessary posting in their transparency boards; send a written notice & submit all monthly reports for BGCM Fund utilization to the DBM; direct the LFC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant; and require the LFC to convene in determination of PPAs and expenses to be sourced from the funds of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Adoption of the following: a. Require the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to timely prepare the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of PPAs for BGCM Fund using the prescribed format (Annex C); b. Require the LFC to post in their transparency boards on a regular basis; c. Send a written notice & submit all monthly reports for BGCM Fund utilization to the DBM and other government oversight offices; d. Direct the LFC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant concerning the allotment, obligations and disbursements for accurate monthly reporting on fund utilization; and e. Require the LFC to convene in the determination of PPAs and expenses to be sourced from the funds of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management update and maintain GAD Database particularly the sex-disaggregated data; and to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Published the GAD Database; the GAD Database updating is still on-going.
observations.reason: Utilization level of GAD Budget was low.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to gather relevant information of the PPE accounts with no accumulated depreciation and immediately provide depreciation to all depreciable assets in accordance with PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The Management backtrack all records pertaining to acquisition costs and assignable property, plant and equipment; plans to keep track of records and acquisition costs and assignable property, plant and equipment.
observations.reason: Lack of inventory team that will backtrack all acquisition records. Inventories were not recorded.

observations.recommendation: 1. Adopt an orderly system or method in the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers to ensure complete portrayal of the details of the fund source for every particular program, project or activity (PPA) where the fund is intended and such other information that are vital for the regular monitoring of the fund receipt, utilization and balances, the status of PPA implementation, monitoring and reporting, and all necessary data required to be reported on are readily available, complete and easily validated. 2. Conduct detailed scrutiny of records to ensure that completed projects and fully liquidated funds with the source agency are properly recorded in the books. 3. Identify appropriate actions to be undertaken for the proper disposition of idled funds. Conduct regular reconciliation of balances with the records of the SA to facilitate early detection and correction of errors.
observations.managementAction: Traced and reviewed all financial transactions affecting the account.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to determine the composition of previous transactions credited to Government Equity account thereafter prepare and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the accounting error in the recording of government financial transactions.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing tracing of this large sum which will require a lot of time and effort.
observations.reason: Tracing this large sum will require a lot of time and effort.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall remit promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal Revenue and strictly adhere with the BIR Regulations to avoid penalties. Moreover, conduct immediate reconciliation of Due to BIR accounts to ensure correctness of the balance reported. Prior years’ balance should be reconstructed/verified to facilitate remittance thereof.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing reconstitution; carry overs from previous reports which cannot be ascertained due to unavailability of records due to the fire which gutted the municipal building in 2004.
observations.reason: Carry overs from previous reports which cannot be ascertained due to the unavailability of records due to the fire which gutted the municipal building in 2004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management observe and comply with the provisions prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160. Appropriations and actual expenses for EME in the ensuing year should be within the limitation.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing compliance with the prescribed provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing; management require the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation for the Municipal Engineer to strictly administer and supervise the implementation and the MPDC to monitor the implementation of development projects. Evaluate the possibility to make targeted amendments to some projects which are no longer feasible or less priority for implementation in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: For the Municipal Engineer to strictly administer and supervise the implementation and the MPDC to monitor the implementation of development projects. Evaluate the possibility to make targeted amendments to some projects which are no longer feasible or less priority for implementation in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Occurrence of natural calamities such as earthquakes and the long dry spell and the unseasonable clearing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 based on the latest Bank Statement; establish the unadjusted discrepancy and regularly prepare an updated Bank Reconciliation Statement in subsequent month while working back and reconciling the prior months’ Bank Reconciliation Statements until such time that the established discrepancy will eventually be identified and corrected accordingly.
observations.managementAction: BRS and Proof of Cash for CY 2011 were prepared for 11 out of the 15 accounts; No BRS was prepared for the 4 bank accounts.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of the monthly BRSs.

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.
observations.managementAction: Made a Job order contract to a person with technical knowledge to assist for this matter.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower; technical assistance required.

observations.recommendation: Officer-in-Charge shall be provided with adequate training for her position to properly discharge her duties and responsibilities and ultimately maintain and submit financial reports for special account.
observations.managementAction: The Officer-in-Charge shall be provided with adequate training for her position to properly discharge her duties and responsibilities and ultimately maintain and submit financial reports for special account.
observations.reason: Time constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the MPDO comply with the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of projects, programs and activities at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as quarterly submission of the required information of PPAs of the Government municipality.
observations.managementAction: Traced and reviewed all financial transactions affecting the account and require the Municipal Engineer and MPDO to comply; publication to the required format is being implemented.
observations.reason: Multi-tasking personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to hire personnel qualified for the vacant positions. Meanwhile, the Officer-in-Charge shall be provided with adequate training for the position to properly discharge their duties and responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: Published to the public and management to hire qualified personnel; Officer-in-Charge provided with training.
observations.reason: No qualified applicant for the mandatory positions.

observations.recommendation: a) Effect the prompt transfer for the cost of completed projects from the Trust Fund to the General Fund; b) Direct the GSO to update the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) as prescribed in Appendix 53 of the Manual on the NGAS to readily identify the Accountable Officers of acquired government property.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing transfer process; GSO to update ARE.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to issue final demand letters to all concerned accountable officers to render full accounting of long outstanding cash advances by submission of fully supported liquidation statements, and/or refund the balance of unused cash advances; b. enjoin all concerned officials and employees to comply with the prescribed timelines on the liquidation of cash advances, and where warranted, enforce the sanctions as maybe appropriate; c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to deny clearance from money accountability to retiring, resigning and transferring personnel who still have unliquidated cash advances; and d. strictly enforce the prohibition on the granting of additional cash advances to those with outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Issued final demand letters, enjoined concerned officials to comply; implemented denial of clearance; prohibition being enforced.
observations.reason: There are unliquidated cash advances in the amount of ₱3,869,790.81 as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively and the Municipal Accountant to establish/adjust the RPT and SET Receivables accounts, accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; the Assessor’s office furnished copy of assessment roll to MTO.
observations.reason: Time constraints; backlog of assessment records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to prepare and submit the required reports under COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 the accounting and reporting of DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; utilization reports were prepared on a quarterly basis only.
observations.reason: Utilization reports were prepared only on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented; management ensured submission of copies of approved contracts and POs within the specified timeframe where feasible.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Team to complete the physical inventory count and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records to validate the existence, conditions and valuation of the said assets. Moreover, submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer maintain and update subsidiary ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each group/class of PPEs.
observations.managementAction: AAR Establish the office of the General Services Officer who will be responsible in implementing its procurement program and managing its property, plant and equipment assets.
observations.reason: Lack of inventory team that will keep track of all government property acquisitions

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation for the Inventory Team to complete the physical
inventory count and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting and property records to
validate the existence, conditions and valuation of the said assets. Moreover, submit the
RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year. We also recommend that
the Municipal Accountant and the Property and Supply Officer maintain and update
subsidiary ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each group/class of PPEs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned
official rectify this accounting practice in order to present fairly the financial statements.
Also, correcting entries should be drawn in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the
accounts affected. Strict compliance to the provision of the IPSAS is also advised.

We recommended and the Management agreed to prepare/issue a memorandum requiring
the responsible personnel to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and
monitor the compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Management institute measures to improve the internal
control on disbursements by requiring the Disbursing Officer to: (1) regularly rule and foot
the cashbooks at the end of each month; (2) coordinate with the Accounting Office and
keep copies of the DVs to be maintained and chronologically filed in the MTO in order to
facilitate the timely and accurate recording of all disbursements in the cashbooks; and (3)
emphasize the strict use of pre-numbered PCVs in the grant and liquidation of PCF.

We recommended that the management require:
a) the Acting Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Budget Officer to (i)
comply with the provisions of IPSAS 24 on the preparation of Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and (ii) reconcile their records on
allotments and obligations monthly so that any resulting discrepancies can be
immediately verified and corrected; and
b) the Acting Municipal Accountant to provide disclosures on the face of the SCBAA
and/or in the Notes to FS the :(i) changes between the original and final budgets;
(ii) material discrepancies between the budget and actual amounts; and (iii)
reconciliation of actual amounts per SCBAA and per financial statements, to
enhance the transparency of the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that management direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to provide
the lacking disclosures and to strictly adhere to the disclosure requirements of IPSAS in
the Notes to Financial Statements to ensure that the financial reports effective
understanding by the intended users.

We recommended that the Management:
a) Coordinate with the DSWD to improve the implementation of succeeding subsidy
programs through timely distribution and liquidation of cash assistance to the
beneficiaries and increase of manpower to facilitate the process flow and activities
involved in the programs; and
b) Direct the concerned offices to timely and accurately prepare the disbursement
voucher and liquidation reports, return the unexpended balance to the concerned
source agency and record the return in the books, as required under the
aforementioned rules and regulations.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the
Management:
a. Require the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to timely and accurately prepare
the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of PPAs
for BGCM Fund using the prescribed format (Annex C), in compliance with
Section 4.2 of LBC No. 125;
b. Require the LFC to accomplish the necessary posting in their transparency boards
on a regular basis to always keep the public informed and updated on the
implementation and utilization of BGCM Fund pursuant to the transparency &
full disclosure policy of the government and Section 4.3 of the said circular;
c. Send a written notice & submit all monthly reports for BGCM Fund utilization to
the DBM and other government oversight offices where the submission of reports
is required under existing laws, rules and regulations, in compliance with Section
4.4 of the said circular;
d. Direct the LFC to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant concerning the
allotment, obligations and disbursements for accurate monthly reporting on fund
utilization; and
e. Require the LFC to convene in the determination of PPAs and expenses to be
sourced from the funds of the LGU in order to properly identify the specific
PPAs and to ensure that disbursements are only made within the limited amounts
and nature of the approved PPAs.

We recommended that the management:
a. update and maintain GAD Database particularly the sex-disaggregated data; and
b. to maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to gather relevant information of the PPE accounts with no accumulated depreciation and immediately provide depreciation to all depreciable assets in accordance with PPSAS.

Direct the GSO to update the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment (ARE) as prescribed in Appendix 53 of the Manual on the NGAS to readily identify the Accountable Officers of acquired government property.
observations.managementAction: Hire a competent manpower. Transfer the cost of completed projects from trust fund to general fund. Create a General Service Office to update ARE.
observations.reason: Lack of competent manpower

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to issue final demand letters to all concerned accountable officers to render full accounting of long outstanding cash advances by submission of fully supported liquidation statements, and/or refund the balance of unused cash advances; b. enjoin all concerned officials and employees to comply with the prescribed timelines on the liquidation of cash advances, and where warranted, enforce the sanctions as maybe appropriate under the afore- cited law, rules and regulations on those who fail to liquidate their cash advances within the reglementary period; c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to deny clearance from money accountability to retiring, resigning and transferring personnel who still have unliquidated cash advances; and d. strictly enforce the prohibition on the granting of additional cash advances to those with outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: a) To issue demand letter. b) Coordinate with the HRMO to remind concerned officials and employees for the timelines of liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/ Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively and the Municipal Accountant to establish /adjust the RPT and SET Receivables accounts, accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Provide/ submit updated document to the municipal treasurer/ accountant. The Assessor’s office to furnish copy of assessment roll to the MTO.
observations.reason: Assessor’s office did not furnish copy of assessment roll to the MTO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to prepare and submit the required reports under COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 the accounting and reporting of DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Trace the document to be able to prepare and submit the required reports. Most of the staff are job orders and there is no permanent employee who may focus on the record since most of the staff are job orders.
observations.reason: Documents cannot be traced due to frequent transfer of office from one place to another and lack of permanent staff.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review, evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.
observations.managementAction: Transfer job order employee from other office to accounting office that will concentrate on this matter and make & submit the Procurement Monitoring Report.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation for the Inventory Team to complete the
physical inventory count and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting and property
records to validate the existence, conditions and valuation of the said assets. Moreover,
submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31st of each year.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to
gather relevant information of the PPE accounts with no accumulated depreciation
and immediately provide depreciation to all depreciable assets in accordance with
PPSAS.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to
make a thorough reconciliation of the books and the records of the creditors in order to
reflect the correct balances of the accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries to
ensure the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to present the
current portion of the Long-Term Loans as of year-end under Loans Payables –
Current Portion in compliance with PPSAS 1 for purposes of fair presentation in
the Agency’s financial statements.

1. Adopt an orderly system or method in the maintenance of subsidiary
ledgers to ensure complete portrayal of the details of the fund source for
every particular program, project or activity (PPA) where the fund is
intended and such other information that are vital for the regular
monitoring of the fund receipt, utilization and balances, the status of PPA
implementation, monitoring and reporting, and all necessary data required
to be reported on are readily available, complete and easily validated.
2. Conduct detailed scrutiny of records to ensure that completed projects and
fully liquidated funds with the source agency are properly recorded in the
books.
3. Identify appropriate actions to be undertaken for the proper disposition of
idled funds. Conduct regular reconciliation of balances with the records of
the SA to facilitate early detection and correction of errors.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to
determine the composition of previous transactions credited to Government Equity
account under the Trust Fund and thereafter prepare and effect the necessary
adjusting journal entries to correct the accounting error in the recording of
government financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall
remit promptly and intact all taxes withheld by the Municipality to the Bureau of Internal
Revenue and strictly adhere with the BIR Regulations to avoid penalties. Moreover,
conduct immediate reconciliation of Due to BIR accounts to ensure correctness of the
balance reported. Prior years’ balance should be reconstructed/verified to facilitate
remittance thereof.

We recommended that management observe and comply with the provisions
prescribed in Sec. 325(h) of RA 7160. Appropriations and actual expenses for
EME in the ensuing year should be within the limitation.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Bids and Award
Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved
by the HOPE and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format
within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We reiterated our recommendation for the Municipal Engineer to strictly
administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and the MPDC to
monitor the implementation of the development projects. Evaluate the possibility to
make amendments to some projects which are no longer feasible or less priority for
implementation in the ensuing year.

We recommended that a start the Office-In-Charge Accountant prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 based on the latest available Bank Statement; establish the unadjusted discrepancy in each depository account as of the specified cut-off date; regularly prepare an updated Bank Reconciliation Statement in subsequent month while working back and reconciling at the same time the prior months’ Bank Reconciliation Statements until such time that the established discrepancy will eventually be identified and corrected accordingly.
observations.managementAction: AAR CPA has been hired to work back on this..
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period.
observations.managementAction: AAR Made a Job order contract 2018 to a person with technical knowledge to assist for this matter.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant and other concerned official rectify this accounting practice in order to present fairly the financial statements. Also, correcting entries should be drawn in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the accounts affected. Strict compliance to Section 114 and Section 121 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Unit (LGU), Volume I is also advised.
observations.managementAction: AAR The management 2018 shifted to expense based accounting, recognising actual supplies with regard to ending inventory figures.

observations.recommendation: Hire personnel with expertise in accounting, preferably Certified Public Accountant (CPA). Meanwhile, the Officer-in-Charge shall be provided with adequate training for her position to properly discharge her duties and responsibilities and ultimately maintain and submit financial reports for special account.
observations.managementAction: AAR Create and hiring of staff to adhere this issue and management endeavors to undertake measures to address this concern with the end in view of presenting a fair and accurate determination of its financial statements.

Submit the list of PPA's at the beginning of the year. Submit the softcopy and hard copy of the list of projects and submit the status of ongoing projects quarterly thru e-mail and hardcopy.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that for a start the Office-In-Charge Accountant prepare Bank
Reconciliation Statements pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 based on the latest
available Bank Statement; establish the unadjusted discrepancy in each depository
account as of the specified cut-off date; regularly prepare an updated Bank
Reconciliation Statement in subsequent month while working back and reconciling at
the same time the prior months’ Bank Reconciliation Statements until such time that the
established discrepancy will eventually be identified and corrected accordingly.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendation that management direct the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and
effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on
the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned official
rectify this accounting practice in order to present fairly the financial statements. Also,
correcting entries should be drawn in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the
accounts affected. Strict compliance to Section 114 and Section 121 of the New
Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Unit (LGU), Volume
I is also advised.

Management comment:
The management already hired a personnel and the OIC-Municipal Accountant
gave assurance that in CY 2019 a separate set of subsidiary ledgers for its special
accounts will be maintained so that adequate and vital information as to the results of
operations of each economic enterprise will be available for monitoring and decision
making purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to hire personnel with expertise in
accounting, preferably Certified Public Accountant (CPA). Meanwhile, the Officer-in-
Charge shall be provided with adequate training for her position to properly discharge
her duties and responsibilities and ultimately maintain and submit financial reports for
special account.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to hire personnel with expertise in
accounting preferably a Certified Public Accountant (CPA). Meanwhile, free the Acting
Accountant from other functions and focus to accounting functions in order to submit
the financial reports in accordance with the aforementioned regulations and strictly
comply with the prescribed period of report submission so that timely audit of account
could be made and avoid sanctions such as withholding of salaries.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and MPDO comply with the
provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list of
projects, programs and activities at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as
the required quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to hire personnel qualified for the vacant
positions. Meanwhile, the Officer-in-Charge shall be provided with adequate training
for the position to properly discharge their duties and responsibilities.

We recommended that the Local School Board to allocate adequate funds for various activities in support of the implementation of the ECCD Program and conduct consultations with local ECCD Council, Child Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD programs.

observations.managementAction: CY – AAR 2015 Findi

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the management to require the Accountant to prepare monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statement. Distribute copy of said BRS to the Bank Manager thru the
Audit Team Leader, together with the paid checks and original copies of the debit/credit
memos, and to the Municipal Treasurer. Record all reconciling items that require
adjusting or correcting entries in the General Ledger to ensure that reconciling items are
properly and immediately attended.

We recommend that management:
1. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count of all
PPEs and inventories and generate a Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE).
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the property, plant
and equipment ledger cards in accordance with Section 120 of the NGAS
manual. In addition, reconcile the inventory report and the ledger cards and
investigate any discrepancy noted.

We recommend that the Municipal Health Office coordinate with the barangay
health centers and determine their needs that can be funded by the PFPR so that the
government’s goal of providing preventive care services to indigents will be fully
implemented, and thus, best results could be achieved in the most opportune time.

We recommend discontinuance of the use of open dump sites and identify possible
sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill
shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the
said act.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the
reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the
correct account balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial
operation as of a given period of time.

We recommend that the management ensure that all copies of approved contracts,
purchase orders and their supporting documents be submitted within five (5) working days
from approval or issuance and within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries for review,
evaluation and inspection of items procured by the Agency.

We recommend that Management intensify efforts in the establishment and
maintenance of a GAD database containing all sex-disaggregated data and gender
statistics, in compliance with the PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and to
ensure that the GAD Focal Person should undergo trainings related to GAD to better equip
her on the task at hand.

We recommend that the Municipal Government adopt a system of payroll payment
through the Automated Teller Machines.

We recommend that the management require the officials concerned to remit promptly the amounts withheld/collected due to other government agencies in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to submit the financial reports in accordance with the aforementioned regulations and strictly comply with the prescribed period of report submission so that timely audit of account could be made.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to require the Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement. Distribute copy of said BRS to the Bank Manager thru the Audit Team Leader, together with the paid checks and original copies of the debit/credit memos, and to the Municipal Treasurer. Record all reconciling items that require adjusting or correcting entries in the General Ledger to ensure that reconciling items are properly and immediately attended.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 3
observations.reason: The non-preparation of BRS of the Accountant was due to lack of personnel to assist him & the delayed in the receipt of BS and returned checks from the local servicing bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPBB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims not duly supported with complete documentary requirements as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, ensure that payment of honoraria shall only be made to successfully completed procurement project for procurement that involves public bidding.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 5

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-2002 dated September 12, 2012 in the accounting and reporting of DRRM fund to ensure that funds are allocated for the DRRM and utilized for the intended purpose, and the same is correctly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurements whenever it grants rice as a form of incentive or benefit to their employees or use rice in connection with their functions. Forge with the NFA an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate the rice procurement arrangement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the members of the BAC should adhere strictly to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184. Ensure that the Invitation to Observers is duly served and properly receipted at least three (3) days before the schedule, to fully use the PhilGEPS and complete required documents is submitted in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 8

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management discontinue the practice of deducting payments on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from the personnel’s payroll. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending establishments must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management implement fully all the targeted priority development projects and programs embodied in the Annual Investment Plan. Strictly administer and supervise the implementation of development projects and likewise monitor its implementation. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate the possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible for implementation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding No. 10
